danger_signals.visual_form: Alien terrain with biomechanical and organic hazards
danger_signals.meaning: Player is stranded in enemy territory; progression requires combat and navigation

danger_signals.visual_form: Diverse enemy types scattered throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Constant threat requiring weapon selection and evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Particularly enormous or swift enemies at world endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger; checkpoint moments requiring pattern recognition and combat skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage timer countdown; health drain upon expiration
danger_signals.meaning: World state becomes actively hostile after time runs out; failure imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Magma chambers, alien hive structures, biomechanical facilities
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats alongside enemy opposition; world is inherently hostile

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible 'remotes' with distinct visual forms (trampoline, helicopter, etc.)
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and progression opportunity; tools for overcoming specific obstacles
Resource gathering for progression; incentive to explore and survive

safety_signals.visual_form: New space-car obtained at end of each world
safety_signals.meaning: Milestone achievement; transition to next world; temporary narrative safety before failure/recapture

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player sprite distinguishes B.O.B. as foreign (human) intruder in alien worlds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Diverse alien enemy designs reflecting world themes
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types indicating faction diversity or world-specific defenders
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemies inhabit domed colonies, hive structures, biomechanical facilities, temples, magma chambers
Appears at game's end as goal destination; represents expected reward

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Space surfboard; sleek robot design; aerial mobility
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears flying past, representing unexpected goal shift and player agency reaffirmation

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Initial stranding - B.O.B. crashed on asteroid
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hostile asteroid filled with diverse enemies; three distinct worlds to traverse
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must fight through multiple level types; vehicle as progress marker

world_state_to_visual.world_state: World 1 and 2 - temporary escape attempts fail
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vehicles break down; new world encountered; visual variety in level design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced into new environments; boss battles mark world transitions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Countdown timer; health drain visual cues upon expiration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World becomes actively lethal; urgency increases; player must move faster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Destination reached; blue robot female appears; narrative conclusion sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player encounters expected goal; conversation/interaction moment; goal rejection sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red female robot flies past on surfboard; visual contrast with blue robot
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player declares new objective; switches pursuit targets; old goal abandoned

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Both characters on small asteroid; space environment; car stalls in background drifting away
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Romantic resolution combined with comedic re-stranding; cycle suggests potential sequel

environmental_storytelling.example: Domed space colony cities, alien hive-type areas, biomechanical facilities, ancient temples, magma chambers — each environment tells story of asteroid's past and current state
First two space-cars fail under comical circumstances, communicating the asteroid's hostility and forcing player deeper into danger rather than allowing escape
Different enemy designs tied to environment themes (hive enemies in biological areas, mechanical enemies in facilities, etc.) suggest ecological or technological faction presence
Particularly enormous or swift enemies at world endpoints mark narrative checkpoints and environmental mastery requirements
Distinct mechanical gameplay segments break up standard platforming, communicating environmental variety and transportation-focused world identity
B.O.B. as human/humanoid stands visually distinct against alien environment, reinforcing stranded outsider narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Weapon deployment; environmental interaction via remotes
Reverts to punch combat; forces ammunition conservation decision-making
Health rapidly drains; movement becomes retreat/panic-driven
Patrol and engage; threat intensity increases with stage progression
Pattern-based attack sequences; requires specific combat strategy; marks environmental mastery checkpoint
Berate and complain; stationary; represents expected romantic reward
Dynamic movement; elicits player goal shift; represents unexpected alternative and player agency

notes: B.O.B. uses comedic narrative framing to communicate world state through repeated failure and goal subversion. Visual variety in level themes (colonies, hives, temples, magma) creates perception of diverse world regions rather than unified faction control. The game's run-and-gun paradigm directly manifests danger through enemy density, boss encounters, and time pressure mechanics. Vehicle acquisition serves dual narrative/mechanical purpose: marks progress while communicating temporary safety before new complications. Character color identity (blue vs. red robots) provides quick visual faction distinction in final narrative sequences. Limited Wikipedia source material provides plot and setting details but lacks specific visual asset descriptions; manifestation patterns inferred from gameplay mechanics and level design concepts described.
